understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to get the movable/selectable piece(s) onto the goal tiles marked with a `+`. If a level had multiple `+` tiles, all of them needed to be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the engine to report WIN. The overall game is completed by winning the final level within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only the currently selected piece responds to actions. Each action (MOVE_* or CLICK) consumes 1 step from the level’s remaining step budget. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece in that direction, but movement can be blocked by walls/obstacles (resulting in no position change). CLICK attempts to move the selected piece directly to the clicked grid coordinate; this succeeds when the destination is a valid reachable/open tile under the level’s movement constraints, and fails (no movement) when the destination is blocked (e.g., a wall/obstacle) or otherwise not a legal reachable target. In the shown run, some clicked targets (e.g., [15,21], [0,21], [18,21]) were legal and caused an immediate reposition, while others (e.g., [21,21], [33,21], [36,18]) were blocked/illegal. Selection itself did not visibly change in these logs, suggesting either there was only one piece or clicks were being used primarily as move-to commands rather than selecting different pieces.

orient_history: Moves apply to the currently selected selectable; CLICK likely changes which selectable is selected (since current piece is immobile).

Hypothesis: CLICK relocates the selected selectable to the target position (even across walls) as long as placement is allowed; goals are non-collidable so placing a selectable on the goal position should satisfy the win condition.

Hypothesis: CLICK teleports the selected 3x3 piece to the clicked pixel position only if the destination aligns to the 3-pixel grid (multiples of 3) and does not collide with walls/other selectables. The goal area likely requires placing a selectable onto an aligned cell within the '+' region.

CLICK likely either (a) selects a piece when clicking on its body, or (b) attempts to reposition/teleport the currently selected piece to the clicked coordinates if that destination is valid (not colliding). MOVE_* moves by one cell (3 pixels) but we’re currently boxed in. To test the CLICK-to-reposition hypothesis, try a clearly cell-aligned point inside the goal region.

CLICK likely interacts with tiles/objects (possibly toggling/removing walls) while MOVE_* shifts the selected piece by 1 cell but is prevented by adjacent walls. Since both pieces appear enclosed, progress may require clicking adjacent wall tiles to open a path toward the goal.

New hypothesis: MOVE_* actions attempt to shift the currently selected piece by one cell (3 pixels). If movement is blocked, the action will fail (no movement). I need to test a MOVE action now to confirm behavior; if it fails, next I should change strategy to selecting/moving the other selectable piece that may have a path to the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK can either (a) select another selectable if clicked on it, or (b) attempt a direct move/teleport to the clicked coordinate when valid; since P is trapped, we should try switching selection to the other piece to see if it has a path or different click-move options toward the goal area.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* only shifts by 3 pixels and is blocked by adjacent collidable walls. CLICK attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked position if it is a valid/reachable landing spot (may bypass some local blocking), and can help explore reachability toward the goal. Unselected selectables persist as separate pieces that may be needed to occupy goals.

CLICK appears to switch which selectable is selected (the log text says 'moved' but the state shows two distinct pieces persisting). Only MOVE_* actually changes a piece’s position when not blocked. To make progress, I should select the other piece and check whether it has any unblocked moves.

CLICK appears to relocate the selected piece directly to certain clicked positions (including the other selectable’s position), even when normal MOVE is blocked. To make progress toward the goal, we should reposition to a location that may allow further movement or a different CLICK path.

Hypothesis: CLICK selects a different selectable piece (setting it as the player), while MOVE_* attempts to move the currently selected piece by one cell (3 px) but is blocked by walls/colliders. To make progress, we must select the other piece at [9,21] to see if it has an open move path toward the goal.

New hypothesis (discarding prior): MOVE_* attempts to shift the currently selected piece by one 3-pixel cell, but both selectable positions are fully enclosed by walls in the current layout, so movement may fail. CLICK seems to relocate selection between existing selectable positions only (not free-move to arbitrary tiles), so I will test whether any MOVE_* can still change state despite the wall reports.

Clicking on a selectable likely changes which piece is selected; movement constraints suggest the currently selected piece is stuck and we must switch to the other piece to make progress/unblock a path.

CLICK seems to change which selectable is controlled (selecting the piece at the clicked coordinates), while MOVE_* shifts the currently selected piece by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked by walls/other selectables.

Hypothesis: CLICK either (a) selects another selectable if clicked on it, or (b) moves/teleports the currently selected piece to the clicked location if it is a valid empty/goal area (possibly not limited to adjacent moves). To solve, we likely need to CLICK a goal tile to place a selectable onto it.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves 1 cell but we are boxed in. CLICK attempts a direct move to the clicked cell but is blocked by intervening walls; alternatively, CLICK on certain walls may toggle/open them. Since there is no apparent corridor, progressing likely requires interacting with the central wall barrier.

CLICK likely allows selecting/switching between selectable pieces (or swapping into an occupied selectable position), which may be necessary since normal movement is currently impossible from the selected piece’s position.

New hypothesis: CLICK teleports/moves the selected piece only within a connected/allowed set of positions (likely to other selectables or reachable cells), but we are currently trapped in a sealed pocket by walls. MOVE actions might be needed to push/slide the adjacent selectable (at [12,21]) to break out, or to test whether movement can force interaction even when ‘blocked’ is reported.

Hypothesis: CLICK on another selectable switches selection (or swaps/moves onto that selectable’s position). To make progress toward the goal, we likely need to control the other selectable at [0,21] to explore a different reachable area or unlock access.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to place/teleport the currently selected 3x3 piece to the exact pixel position clicked, but the move fails if the destination overlaps any collidable wall/occupied space. The goal area may not be aligned to the 3-pixel grid (goal object is at [35,17]), so clicking grid-aligned positions like [36,18] may have overlapped walls, while clicking the goal’s exact top-left may succeed.

CLICK appears to reposition the selected 3x3 (1-cell) piece directly to the clicked pixel coordinate if that top-left aligns to the 3-pixel grid and does not overlap a wall/occupied tile; intervening walls do not matter. The goal click failed because [35,17] is not aligned to multiples of 3. We should click an aligned pixel coordinate inside the goal cells (e.g., [33,15] for cell (11,5)).

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active when clicking on another selectable; arrow MOVEs then attempt to move the currently selected piece within open space (if any). Since current piece cannot move, we must switch selection to test the other piece's mobility toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK attempts to move the selected piece directly to the clicked pixel position if that destination is not colliding with a wall (and possibly if the route/line is valid), while MOVE_* is a 1-cell (3px) step that is currently blocked by adjacent wall collisions. To reach the goal, we likely need to use CLICK to reposition to an intermediate open coordinate near the central wall, then route around it.

Discarding prior click-teleport-only strategy. New hypothesis: MOVE actions may have an effect beyond immediate translation when adjacent cells are walls (e.g., attempt to slide/resolve to nearest valid cell, or potentially switch control/selection when movement is blocked). I have only observed MOVE_LEFT once with no position change, so I will probe other MOVE directions to test for any non-obvious behavior.

Arrow moves are currently impossible from this position due to surrounding walls. CLICK likely attempts to relocate the selected piece to the clicked cell if that destination is valid (e.g., a goal/empty tile) and not colliding; previous clicks may have failed due to targeting non-aligned/invalid cells. Goal cells in semantic correspond to pixel multiples of 3 around x=33..39, y=15..21.

Hypothesis: CLICK on a selectable's position can change which piece is selected (and/or move the currently selected piece only when a valid slide/teleport target exists). Since movement is fully blocked for the current selected piece, we should try selecting the other piece to attempt reaching the goal.

CLICK likely changes which selectable is active when clicking on another selectable’s position; movement via MOVE_* is grid-based (3-pixel steps) and blocked by walls. Need to get both selectables onto goal area; first I must gain control of the other selectable to see if it has mobility toward the goal.

Hypothesis: MOVE_* moves the currently selected selectable by 3 pixels (1 cell) if not blocked by collidable walls. CLICK does not teleport; it switches which selectable is selected if you click on an existing selectable’s position. To make progress, we must select the other piece and see if it has any available moves toward the goal.

Hypothesis: CLICK on an existing selectable changes which piece is selected/controlled. To make progress with low steps, we should control the other selectable at [18,21] and attempt to place/move it onto the goal region (possibly via a follow-up CLICK on a goal cell).

New hypothesis (discarding old): CLICK does not perform pathfinding to the goal; it only swaps/teleports between specific selectable anchors, and movement is constrained by collision. With the currently selected piece boxed in by walls, only a MOVE action can test whether any direction has a hidden passage despite the 'blocked' flags (or confirm no movement possible).

CLICK appears to either (a) move the selected piece to a clicked empty location when permitted by the movement rules, or (b) select/swap focus with the other selectable when clicking its position. Previous CLICK into the goal corner [33,15] did not move, suggesting line-of-sight/path constraints may prevent entering the enclosed goal area. With no MOVE possible, the only remaining chance is a CLICK directly inside the goal region in case it allows placement despite walls.

Movement consumes steps; when steps reach 0, no further meaningful movement/clicks can be executed. The selected piece is currently enclosed by walls, and the goal is elsewhere; with 0 steps left, the level cannot be completed from this state.

history.summary: Level 1 step budget exhausted.
Agent explained goal and mechanics.

understanding.goal_understanding: Each level’s objective was to get the movable/selectable piece(s) onto the goal tiles marked with a `+`. If a level had multiple `+` tiles, all of them needed to be occupied by selectable pieces at the same time for the engine to report WIN. The overall game is completed by winning the final level within the per-level step budget.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Only the currently selected piece responds to actions. Each action (MOVE_* or CLICK) consumes 1 step from the level’s remaining step budget. MOVE_LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN attempts to move the selected piece in that direction, but movement can be blocked by walls/obstacles (resulting in no position change). CLICK attempts to move the selected piece directly to the clicked grid coordinate; this succeeds when the destination is a valid reachable/open tile under the level’s movement constraints, and fails (no movement) when the destination is blocked (e.g., a wall/obstacle) or otherwise not a legal reachable target. In the shown run, some clicked targets (e.g., [15,21], [0,21], [18,21]) were legal and caused an immediate reposition, while others (e.g., [21,21], [33,21], [36,18]) were blocked/illegal. Selection itself did not visibly change in these logs, suggesting either there was only one piece or clicks were being used primarily as move-to commands rather than selecting different pieces.
